turns.user_message: I woke up with this heavy weight pressing right behind my collarbones, which honestly used to just fade away after my morning coffee. Now it just sits there, making every single breath feel like I am dragging a backpack full of bricks. I keep trying to stretch it out and roll my shoulders back, but the tension refuses to budge even a little bit. It is just a weird, dull ache that makes me want to curl up on the floor instead of facing the day. I really wish it would just go back to being a passing thing like it always was before.

That same tightness has completely taken over my routine and refuses to leave me alone for more than a few minutes at a time. I catch myself rubbing the exact same spot on my back until the skin feels raw, hoping the friction will somehow break the cycle. It used to be something I could easily ignore while I pushed through my usual tasks, but now it dictates how I move. Every hour drags by while this heavy pressure builds up, leaving me completely drained before I even get halfway through my schedule. I am just so tired of fighting a battle against my own body that I never signed up for.

The ache has morphed into a relentless, grinding exhaustion that pins me to my chair and makes even blinking feel like a chore. I try to force myself upright, but my muscles just lock up and refuse to listen to any of my commands. What used to be a minor annoyance has completely taken over my entire frame, leaving me hollowed out and utterly spent. I sit here staring at the wall while my chest feels like it is wrapped in thick, unbreakable iron bands. There is absolutely no energy left in me to pretend I am fine when my whole body is practically collapsing under the weight.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by describing a physical feeling. This used to not bother the speaker.
